text_content: 201.10.101.3.5 LEAKAGE RADIATION under fault conditions Means shall be provided to TERMINATE IRRADIATION if the ELECTRON BEAM is not correctly incident on the TARGET or ELECTRON RADIATION window. In the area defined in 201.10.101.3.3, when the ABSORBED DOSE RATE due to LEAKAGE RADIATION outside the area M exceeds five times the limits SPECIFIED in 201.10.101.3.3, TERMINATION OF IRRADIATION shall occur. The ABSORBED DOSE RATE due to LEAKAGE RADIATION shall be averaged over not more than 10 s and expressed as a percentage of the REFERENCE ABSORBED DOSE. The methodology and test results shall be included in the ACCOMPANYING DOCUMENTATION. text_content: A comprehensive risk study has been conducted for HAL 4.0 in accordance with ISO 14971:2019 (1) and Varian’s Risk Management Process. The study was conducted by cross-functional teams consisting of the members identified in Table 1. The teams reviewed risk to health posed by all persons interacting with HAL 4.0. This analysis considered hazards that could occur both in normal use (or foreseeable misuse) and hazards associated with potential system or sub-system failures. Hazards, hazardous situations, and harms have been identified using SHaF (System Hazard Framework). The hazards, hazardous situations, and harms, applicable to HAL 4.0 are documented in HAL 4.0 Elaboration Phase RHA (10). The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations have been assessed and risk control measures have been identified. The risks associated with these hazards and hazardous situations as well as the risk control measures are documented in this report in Appendix B: Risk Assessment.

source: D59555 HAL 4.0 Risk Management Plan rev03.pdf
title: 4.0 Requirements for Risk Management Activities and their Review (Continued)
text_content: FMEA sessions require a cross-functional and technically biased team approach. FMEA analysis relies on domain knowledge of the team to identify components to consider and specific manner or way by which a failure occurs.  Analysis is conducted thru brainstorming sessions by the team.   Manner or way by which a failure occurs is highly domain (mechanical, electrical, or software) specific. Process FMEA investigates the risk in manufacturing and the supply chain that may cause material or product not meeting requirements, manufacturing operation impact or manufacturing operator impact. Contributing documents such as specifications, requirements, reports, investigations, and studies shall be reviewed by persons with recorded competence in the documents’ subjects. Where relevant, these persons shall have knowledge and experience with the device(s) in this RMP’s scope as well as familiarity with the device’s use(s) and technologies.
